tweets.full_text:
プログラミング未経験の私が
Claude Codeで初めてプログラムを動かしました🐃

まず最初に何をすべきか調べたら
「Hello Worldを表示させてみろ」とのこと。

実はこれ、プログラミングの世界では
"最初にやる儀式"らしいです。

「Hello Worldと表示して」と
日本語で話しかけただけ

→ AIが自動でコードを書いてくれて
→ 画面に Hello World が表示された🎉

コードを一文字も書いてないです笑

これがAI時代のプログラミングなのですね！

#ClaudeCode #プログラミング初心者 #AI活用

tweets.full_text:
やった！！Claude Code v2.1.114 ついに起動成功！！！

この8bit風のスタート画面見た瞬間、小学生時代がフラッシュバックした…
銭湯の風呂上がり、瓶のフルーツ牛乳をグビグビ飲みながら、脱衣所の隅に置いてあったインベーダーゲームをこっそりのぞき見してたあの感じ。

オレンジのピクセルインベーダー、ふわふわ浮かぶ雲、C型の月、星空…まさに「ようこそ」って言われてるみたいで、ログイン成功の緑文字が出た瞬間、胸が熱くなった。

1980年代のゲームセンターや駄菓子屋の匂いがするこのレトロ感。
これから毎日、この相棒と一緒にコード書くのが楽しみで仕方ない！
ワクワク！！

#ClaudeCode #Claude #AIcoding #レトロゲーム

中小企業診断士登録予定｜同じ温度感の方、気軽に繋がりましょう。

tweets.full_text:
ぶっちゃけ月15万ぐらい狙うなら、
Claude Codeにこの指示するだけでOKです。

エンジニアじゃなくても、AIに開発を手伝わせるだけで小さく収益化できる方法を公開します↓

① 作るツールをClaude Codeに決めさせる

まずこう聞きます。

「個人開発で作りやすくて需要があるミニWebサービスを初心者向けに30個出して」

例
・SNS投稿生成ツール
・文章リライトツール
・ブログタイトル生成ツール
・AI要約ツール
・PDFまとめツール

→この中から
「これなら作れそう」なものを選びます。

個人開発で一番大事なのは

“作れるもの”より
“使われるもの”を選ぶこと。

⸻

② Claude Codeに設計させる

次にこう聞きます。

「このサービスの最小構成（MVP）を設計して」

・必要機能
・ページ構成
・データ構造
・技術スタック

→これだけで
サービス設計がほぼ完成します。

⸻

③ 開発タスクを全部分解させる

次にこう聞きます。

「このサービスを1週間で作るタスクに分解して」

例

Day1
環境構築

Day2
API作成

Day3
UI作成

→これで
何を作ればいいか迷わなくなります。

個人開発が止まる原因の多くは
作業順が曖昧なことです。

⸻

④ Claude Codeに実装を書かせる

次にこう指示します。

「この機能のコードを書いて」
「初心者でも理解できる形にして」
「コメント付きにして」

→Claude Codeは
コード生成の精度がかなり高いです。

ゼロから書くより
生成コードを修正する方が速いです。

⸻

⑤ サービスとして公開する

作ったツールは

・Webアプリ公開
・Chrome拡張
・APIツール

として出します。

小さなツールでも
一定の需要があります。

⸻

⑥ 収益化の方法

例えば
・広告（AdSense）
・サブスク（月500円など）
・API課金
・有料版機能

など。

小さいツールでも
ユーザーが増えると収益になります。

⸻

⑦ 案件化する

ツールを作った経験は
そのまま仕事になります。

例
・ツール開発案件
・AIツール制作代行
・社内ツール作成

→実績があるだけで
案件が取りやすくなります。

⸻

⑧ 横展開する

Claude Codeで

・ツール1
・ツール2
・ツール3

と増やしていくと
収入が安定してきます。

個人開発は

1発当てるより
数を作る方が成功率が高いです。

⸻

やることまとめ

・Claude Codeにアイデア出させる
・設計させる
・タスク分解させる
・コード生成させる
・ツール公開する
・収益化する

正直かなりシンプルです。

⸻

どれくらい狙えるのか？

月500円 × 300人
＝ 月15万円

または

ツール開発案件
5万円 × 月3件
＝ 月15万円

⸻

Claude Codeの強みは

「コードを書くAI」ではなく
「開発を一緒に進めるAI」なこと。

個人開発で強い人は
天才エンジニアというより

作れるプロダクト数が多い人です。

tweets.full_text:
SalesforceがUIを全部捨てて、AIエージェント専用のインフラに生まれ変わった

TDX 2026で発表されたHeadless 360はCRMの巨人がブラウザ不要に舵を切った瞬間で、SaaSの歴史的な転換点だと思ってる

①60以上のMCPツールを一気に公開
Claude Code・Cursor・Codexが Salesforce のデータ・ワークフローに直接アクセスできるようになった。ダッシュボードを開かなくてもターミナルからCRMを操作できる

②課金モデルをユーザー単位から従量制に変更
AIエージェントが仕事をする前提だと、1人あたり月額いくらって考え方自体が破綻する。Salesforceが自分からその課金モデルを壊しにいった

③Slack・Teams・ChatGPT・Claudeから直接操作
営業がSalesforceにログインして手で入力する時代が終わる。Slackで指示すればエージェントが勝手にCRMを更新してくれる

うちもClaude Codeで業務の大半を回してるけど、SaaSの巨人が自らUIを捨てに来たのはAIファースト経営のティッピングポイント

リプ欄↓

tweets.full_text:
また、現場エンジニアが一人抜けた。

AIエージェント導入、あと2年で『人間不要』が現実になる。

Claude CodeとCodexを内側から触ってきた立場から書く。

今の開発現場、静かに壊れてる。

支えてるのは、ツールの仕様を独学で追いかける末端エンジニアと、承認フローを整備しないまま自動化を押し進める経営層と、月数万円のAPIコストを自腹で吸収するフリーランス。

3方向から、同時に崩れてる。

Claude Coworkがエンタープライズに降りてきた。Codexがエージェントとして自律でPRを出す。MCPのトランケーション上限まで現場が手動で調整してる。

誰が設定を覚えてると思ってる?

Claude Codeの権限設計、どこまで人間が見るか。これ、まだ誰も正解を持ってない。

Terminalに流れるログを毎日追えてる開発者、世界でどれだけいる?

ほとんどの組織、ゼロだよ。

まず個人開発者が限界を超え、次にスタートアップの開発チームが溶け、最後に大企業の内製部隊が空洞化する。

あなたのチームの『AIわかる人』、来年もそこにいる?

経営は『効率化』と資料に書く。エンジニアの離職票には『業務の変質』と書かれてる。

誰かの消耗を燃料にした自動化、それを進歩って呼んでいいのか。

https://t.co/vcJpUXEeZo

#AIエージェント #ClaudeCode #Codex

tweets.full_text:
Wait. You can now trigger Codex FROM Claude Code?

Let me say that again.

You open Claude Code, have Opus think deeply through the architecture, nail down the hard decisions — then hand the fast, repetitive edits straight to Codex.

Two AI agents. One workflow. Each doing what it does best.

This is not just a cool trick. This is what the future of human-AI collaboration actually looks like — not one model doing everything, but a thoughtfully designed team where roles are clear, speed is real, and YOU stay in the director's chair.

Think about it: senior engineers have always known that good architecture thinking and fast execution are different skills. Now your AI stack can reflect that same wisdom.

The developers who figure this out early are not just saving hours. They are building a completely different relationship with how work gets done.

If you have been treating AI tools as single-player utilities, this is your moment to think in systems.

The ceiling just moved.

https://t.co/fDZHNt7V2g

#ClaudeCode #Codex #AIAgents #DeveloperTools #FutureOfWork #Anthropic #OpenAI
@JustSteveKing Its actually almost all Claude code after the empty darkness. I hadn't been coding much and Claude code brought me right back. I feel like an engineer again.

tweets.full_text:
I just mass-reverted every Claude Code project back to Opus 4.6.

Here's why.

I've been running Opus 4.7 for the past few days across multiple production builds. Lead routing platform. Case Cleanse. Real software that ships to real clients.

And something was off.

Tasks that Opus 4.6 would knock out in 10 to 15 minutes were taking over an hour. Sometimes 90 minutes. Same prompts. Same project structure. Same everything.

It would eventually finish. But the output was worse.

More hallucinated file paths. More circular refactoring that went nowhere. More "let me restructure this for you" when nobody asked for a restructure.

I'm not a casual user. I've pushed billions of tokens through Claude Code building full production platforms. Hundreds of sessions. I know what good output looks like from this tool because I've been living inside it every single day.

So I rolled back to Opus 4.6 with the 1 million token context window.

First task after the revert? Done in 12 minutes. Clean code. No detours.

Here's how to do it yourself if you're running into the same issues.

The quick way is to just type /model inside any Claude Code session and select claude-opus-4-6 from the list.

But if you want it locked in across all your projects, add it to your settings.json:

{ "model": "claude-opus-4-6-1m" }
Important detail here. If you just set it to claude-opus-4-6 you're going to get the default 200k context window. That works fine for smaller tasks but if you're running complex multi-file builds you want the full million tokens. The 1M model string is claude-opus-4-6-1m. That "max" in the model name is what unlocks the extended context. Don't skip it.

The lesson here isn't "new model bad." It's that newer doesn't automatically mean better for your specific workflow. Everyone's chasing the latest model drop like it's a sneaker release. Meanwhile the people actually shipping software are benchmarking against their own output, not a leaderboard.

If you're building with AI every day, you need to treat model selection the same way you'd treat any other infrastructure decision. Test it. Measure it. And don't be afraid to downgrade if the numbers tell you to.

The best model is the one that ships your product.

tweets.full_text:
Another AI agent team, gone quiet.

Claude Code, Codex, Gemini Agent Mode — one of these stacks will swallow enterprise comms whole within 3 years.

I run AI agent workflows for PR and IR. Been inside this shift for two years now.

The infrastructure beneath it? Already cracking.

Three load-bearing pillars: uptime guarantees no vendor actually honors, model versioning that silently breaks prod overnight, governance controls bolted on after the fact.

All three buckling at once.

— — —

Here is what the cracks look like up close.

Claude went dark on April 15. No warning. API, https://t.co/tFsFfcRCJA, Claude Code — all hit simultaneously. Zero pre-incident comms. IR teams discovered it through user complaints, not vendor alerts.

Opus 4.6 was silently pulled from Claude Desktop's Code tab the moment 4.7 shipped. No changelog. No migration window. Enterprises running validated configs woke up to a different model.

Claude Cowork GA dropped analytics and spend controls — features that should have shipped on day one of any serious enterprise rollout, not as a retrofit after companies already went live.

This is not a product maturity story. This is a governance vacuum story.

— — —

A single API token, pushed to prod at 2 a.m.

No human reviewed it. No rollback was staged. That token touches your earnings release draft before your IR counsel does. Sit with that.

— — —

Every other major cloud vendor publishes SLA commitments, model deprecation timelines, and incident pre-notification protocols. AI agent platforms are operating without any of that baseline.

This is not standard enterprise software behavior. This is a different category of risk dressed up in a productivity pitch.

— — —

First it hits early adopters who moved fast. Then it reaches the mid-market teams who followed their lead. Then it lands inside the regulated industries — finance, pharma, public companies — where a silent model swap is not an ops inconvenience. It is a disclosure event.

Your IR workflow running on an agent stack right now — do you know which model version signed off on that language this morning?

— — —

Vendors say 'agentic enterprise.' Compliance teams say 'we were never consulted.' The gap between those two sentences is where the liability lives.

Convenience built on invisible infrastructure failures is not innovation. It is debt. And someone's audit committee is going to collect it.

https://t.co/sWvB9mMSt3

#AIAgent #ClaudeCode #EnterpriseAI #IRRisk #Anthropic #OpenAI #Codex #GeminiAgent #TechGovernance
What if next version of GPT model is as good as Claude in design. Will you ditch Claude Code?

quoted_status.full_text:
How to customize AI agenst — Codex and Claude Code — for academic work:

Step 1: Learn to write “agents md” and “claude md” files

What are “agents md” and “claude md” files and why do you need to write them?

“agents md” and “claude md” are a set of instructions that Codex and Claude Claude Code use every time they start. Codex uses an “agents md” file while Claude Code uses “claude md.”

Codex and Claude Code are AI agents and need to be installed on your computer unlike browser-based apps like ChatGPT and Claude.

Every time you start Codex or Claude Code, they automatically load any file named “agents md” or “claude md” respectively. 

What is an md file?

An md or a markdown file is a simple text file that you can create in a text editor like Notepad. Create a file in Notepad and save it with an md extension and it will become an md file.

Your “agents md ” or “claude md” file should contain your requirements, preferences, and prohibitions. 

Think of these files as a briefing document that you would share with your research assistant soon after hiring them. 

You’d tell your research assistant what their duties are and how you want them to go about their job. 

You want your research assistant to memorize this initial set of instructions. You don’t want to brief your research assistant every morning.

A precise and well-written “agents md” or “claude md” file will turn your AI agent into an extremely powerful assistant that is customized according to your specific requirements.

How to organize your “agent md” or “claude md” file

The first thing to keep in mind while writing your “agents md” or “claude md” is to be precise and concise. 

Don’t start with a very lengthy set of instructions. Keep it limited to one page only.

The function of an “agents md” or “claude md” is not to anticipate every single request but to give your AI agent a set of parameters and framework to work within.

You can organize your “agents md” or “claude md” file in the following five sections:

1. Role
Start with describing the kind of academic work you have done in the past and what your current interests are. The more specific you are, the better. Then describe what you expect from Claude Code.

Writing tip: Write imperative sentences: do this, don’t do that, etc. For example, “Cite only published articles.”

2. Tasks
List the kind of tasks you want to assign to Claude Code. Try not to niche down here and make the tasks overly specific. Describe the task broadly but try not to be too vague. 

For example, instead of saying that you would need Claude to help you with brainstorming research ideas about your current project, ask it to help you with brainstorming questions in your field of research.

Writing tip: You will need to be both broad and specific while describing your tasks. If you write about your tasks broadly, try not to be too vague about it. 

And if you write specifically about a certain kind of task, try not to be too niche. You’ll need to strike a balance. But don’t worry about it too much in the beginning.

3. Protocols for Recurring Tasks
For recurring tasks, you should write a protocol or a set of instructions. For example, if you write a lot of zero drafts (free writing) and want Claude Code to clean them up, write a step-by-step protocol that Claude Code should follow.

Writing tip: If you have multiple recurring tasks, don’t describe every single detail about them in the main file. This will make your main file too unwieldy. 

Instead, create another file (e.g. “dataextraction md”) in which describe the recurring task in detail. In the main file, refer to that file while describing your recurring task.

4. Communication Style
AI apps often produce verbose, long-winded sentences. Tell your AI agent to write precise and concise sentences and to be as economical with words as possible. You can also ask it to not be deferential and to analyze your comments critically.

Writing tip: Forbid what you don’t want your agent to do. For example, “never fabricate references,” “never include fluff in your responses,” etc. 

5. Writing Style
You can make your agent mimic your prose style. To do so, describe your style, voice, and include a sample.
 
Writing tip: You can include a sample within the file, or you can create another md file titled “mywritingstyle” and refer to it in your main file.

Iterate
Your first claude md or agents md files will not be perfect. So, don’t spend too much time on them. 

Try to keep them between 500-800 words. Give your agent a task, evaluate its response, and the edit the file accordingly.

まず個人、次に小チーム、最後に全社がエージェント依存で止まる。あなたのチームの運用ガイド、今夜も存在しないままでは?

tweets.full_text:
Built goBrain — a local-first unified second brain for AI conversations.

Every session from Claude Code, Claude web/desktop/mobile, Grok, and autonomous agents lands in one markdown vault + pgvector index. A `search_brain` MCP tool queries everything from wherever you work.

Stack: Postgres 16 + pgvector HNSW (768-dim), Ollama running Gemma 4 E2B (rerank) + E4B (summarize) + nomic-embed-text, optional Qwen 3.5 35B-A3B MoE on llama.cpp for the heavy tier. FastAPI ingester watches Claude Code JSONLs, inbox drops for Grok/Claude.ai zips, and any HTTP POST. Graceful fallback to raw ANN when the GPU is contended.

MIT, fully local. Runs on a Mac Mini + NAS.

https://t.co/sW9gw57mEv
